title: 10x Protocols: Chromium Next GEM Single Cell 5' -- University of Minnesota TMCs (CG000331 Rev F)
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"7dh7","text":" Following single-cell dissociation or nuclei isolation, the Next GEM 5' assay uses microfluidics to partition and assign cell- or nuclei-specific barcodes to transcript cDNA at the 5' end. Barcoded cDNA is prepared with adaptors for sequencing by synthesis (SBS). The following protocol has been used at the University of Minnesota TMCs in collaboration with the University of Minnesota Genomics Center. The following protocol has been adapted from protocols developed by 10x Genomics and Illumina to be used at the University of Minnesota TMCs in collaboration with the University of Minnesota Genomics Center. These protocols are owned by their respective companies and are subject to periodic revision.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"i287","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: CG000331_Chromium_Next_GEM_Single_Cell_5_v2_UserGuide_RevF.pdf Sequence with the read format 29,10,10,89 Sequencers used at UMN Genomics Center: Illumina NextSeq 2000 Illumina NovaSeq 6000 Illumina NovaSeq X Plus
BCL data from Illumina sequencer is demultiplexed and converted into FASTQ format using bcl2fastq version 2.20.0
Complete single cell or nuclei isolation prior to starting this protocol
creator: Allie Abdellatif
